message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-038 (fix) in tests/test_docs_drift.py
What was done: docs-drift guards made two-sided (exact GOVERNANCE off-box phrase, baseline attribution present, all five ADR-0006 extras + no "four extras").
Regression test(s): test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier, test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest, test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this fix. Let me start by examining the changed file and the regression tests.

content.content:
1	"""Docs-vs-code drift guards (pass-2 #136, #150, #151, #152, #153, #154).
2	
3	Normative docs make claims a public reader relies on. These guards pin the corrected wording so
4	a regression (re-introducing an over-claim or a stale status) fails CI. They assert on the
5	*specific* stale strings the pass-2 audit found, plus presence of the honest replacement.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
13	
14	
15	def _read(rel: str) -> str:
16	    return (_ROOT / rel).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
17	
18	
19	def test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier():
20	    # #136 + #F-038: the absolute "nothing leaves the machine" over-claim must be qualified — the
21	    # optional high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box. Pin the SPECIFIC high-stakes caveat
22	    # phrase ("not metered, but off-box"), not a loose "off-box" substring: GOVERNANCE has a
23	    # second, unrelated "off-box" occurrence (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so the loose
24	    # check passed even if the high-stakes-tier caveat itself were deleted.
25	    for rel in ("README.md", "docs/CHARTER.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
26	        text = _read(rel)
27	        assert "nothing leaves the machine" not in text, f"{rel}: unconditional over-claim (#136)"
28	        # each file must tie the off-box caveat to the metered/subscription distinction
29	        assert "off-box" in text and "metered" in text, (
30	            f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box / metered caveat missing (#136)"
31	        )
32	    # GOVERNANCE has a SECOND, unrelated "off-box" (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so a
33	    # loose substring passed even if the high-stakes-tier caveat were deleted. Pin its exact
34	    # localized phrase so deleting it fails (#F-038).
35	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
36	    assert "not metered, but off-box" in gov, (
37	        "GOVERNANCE high-stakes off-box caveat removed (#F-038)"
38	    )
39	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0003-local-first-data-sovereign.md")
40	    assert "off-box" in adr and "the same as" in adr  # billing vs residency separated
41	
42	
43	def test_charter_status_is_not_stale():
44	    # #150: the status line must not still call the seams "stubs being wired".
45	    charter = _read("docs/CHARTER.md")
46	    assert "stubs being wired" not in charter
47	    assert "implemented and composed" in charter or "in place and hardened" in charter
48	
49	
50	def test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest():
51	    # #151 + #F-038: the engine ships primitives + a determinism harness, NOT embedding/clustering/
52	    # graph builders — those are adapter-supplied. Two-sided: assert the POSITIVE attribution
53	    # (the choice-bearing builders come from the adapter's BaselineBuilder, not the engine), so a
54	    # regression re-introducing an "engine ships builders" claim — which leaves "BaselineBuilder"
55	    # in the text and so passed the old single-substring check — now fails.
56	    arch = _read("docs/ARCHITECTURE.md")
57	    init = _read("src/assay_engine/__init__.py")
58	    for text in (arch, init):
59	        assert "BaselineBuilder" in text
60	    assert "not shipped by the engine" in arch, "ARCHITECTURE missing the explicit non-ship caveat"
61	    assert "supplied per study by" in init, "__init__ missing the adapter-attribution caveat"
62	
63	
64	def test_preregistration_timestamp_wording_matches_shipped_default():
65	    # #152: body text must not present RFC-3161 as the in-force mechanism; HMAC is the default.
66	    for rel in ("docs/METHODOLOGY.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
67	        text = _read(rel)
68	        assert "HMAC" in text, f"{rel}: shipped HMAC default not stated (#152)"
69	        assert "pluggable" in text, f"{rel}: RFC-3161 must be described as pluggable (#152)"
70	    # Guard the SPECIFIC stale over-claims, so re-introducing either one fails CI even though the
71	    # word "HMAC" appears elsewhere in the file (file-level substring checks alone don't catch a
72	    # localized regression — the pass-2 confirmation flagged this on GOVERNANCE §2 step 3).
73	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
74	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority" not in gov, (
75	        "GOVERNANCE §2 step 3 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
76	    )
77	    meth = _read("docs/METHODOLOGY.md")
78	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped before confirmation" not in meth, (
79	        "METHODOLOGY §7 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
80	    )
81	
82	
83	def test_readme_install_notes_not_on_pypi():
84	    # #153: the quickstart must not present `pip install assay-engine` as a working path without
85	    # the not-yet-on-PyPI caveat + a from-source path.
86	    readme = _read("README.md")
87	    assert "Not yet on PyPI" in readme
88	    assert "pip install -e" in readme  # the working from-source path
89	
90	
91	def test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra():
92	    # #154 + #F-038: ADR-0006's extras enumeration must include `baseline` AND must not understate
93	    # the count. Two-sided: assert every current extra name is present (so dropping any one fails)
94	    # and that no stale "four extras" undercount survives alongside.
95	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md")
96	    for extra in (
97	        "`reasoning`",
98	        "`observability`",
99	        "`persistence`",
100	        "`orchestration`",
101	        "`baseline`",
102	    ):
103	        assert extra in adr, f"ADR-0006 extras enumeration missing {extra} (#F-038)"
104	    assert "four extras" not in adr, "ADR-0006 understates the extras count (stale 'four extras')"
105

content.text: All three pass. Now let me verify discrimination: confirm the diff that the fix introduced, to ensure these are genuinely two-sided and the old version would have passed despite a reverted doc.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show ba82429 -- tests/test_docs_drift.py | head -120

content.content:
commit ba82429c27cc3905c6733517630d39e33795a242
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Tue Jun 16 22:29:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p3): batch 6 — two-sided docs-drift guards (F-038)
    
    Pass-3 confirmed finding (2-agent verified, revert-discriminated):
    
    - F-038 the docs-drift guards were one-sided (presence-only), so a regression that
      ADDED a stale claim alongside the corrected text passed:
      * data-sovereignty: GOVERNANCE has a second, unrelated "off-box" occurrence (SaaS
        observability disqualification), so the loose substring passed even with the
        high-stakes-tier caveat deleted. Now pins the exact localized phrase
        "not metered, but off-box".
      * baseline-toolkit scope: asserted only "BaselineBuilder" present; now also asserts
        the positive attribution ("not shipped by the engine" / "supplied per study by"),
        so re-introducing an "engine ships builders" claim fails.
      * ADR-0006 extras: asserts every current extra name is enumerated and that no stale
        "four extras" undercount survives.
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 498 passed, 5 skipped.
    F-038 revert-discrimination verified (deleting the GOVERNANCE caveat fails the test).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

-        assert "off-box" in text, f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box caveat missing (#136)"

+    # GOVERNANCE has a SECOND, unrelated "off-box" (the SaaS-observability disqualification), so a

     assert "off-box" in adr and "the same as" in adr  # billing vs residency separated

content.text: Now let me verify the discrimination claim directly: revert the GOVERNANCE caveat and confirm the test fails. Let me check the actual GOVERNANCE line 132 context to confirm "not metered, but off-box" is the high-stakes-tier-specific phrase and not duplicated elsewhere.

content.content:
All computation, storage, tracing, experiment tracking, and the local **bulk** reasoning tier
run **on-box** — no data, embeddings, traces, or results from those paths leave the machine.
SaaS observability and any service that would ship analyzed content off-box are disqualified by
this constraint, independent of cost. The one deliberate exception is the optional **high-stakes**
reasoning tier, which sends prompt content to a frontier model via a fixed-cost subscription
(not metered, but off-box); a study handling data that must never leave the box uses only the
bulk tier. This is a billing-vs-residency distinction: "no metered API" is not the same as
"on-box".

content.text:
The discrimination claim is fully verified. Line 129 has the unrelated SaaS "off-box" occurrence; line 132 has the high-stakes-tier-specific "not metered, but off-box". A revert that deleted the high-stakes caveat but left line 129 would have passed the old loose `"off-box" in text` check, but now fails the exact-phrase assertion.

Let me do a quick empirical revert-discrimination to be conclusive.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && cp docs/GOVERNANCE.md /tmp/gov.bak && cp docs/ARCHITECTURE.md /tmp/arch.bak && cp docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md /tmp/adr.bak
# 1) delete high-stakes caveat line but keep SaaS off-box line
perl -0pi -e 's/ \(not metered, but off-box\)//' docs/GOVERNANCE.md
# 2) remove non-ship caveat from ARCHITECTURE
perl -0pi -e 's/, not shipped by the engine//' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
# 3) drop the baseline extra from ADR-0006 enumeration & add stale "four extras"
perl -0pi -e 's/`orchestration`,\n   `baseline`/`orchestration`) under four extras/' docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md
.venv/bin/pytest tests/test_docs_drift.py::test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier tests/test_docs_drift.py::test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest tests/test_docs_drift.py::test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra -q 2>&1 | tail -25
# restore
cp /tmp/gov.bak docs/GOVERNANCE.md && cp /tmp/arch.bak docs/ARCHITECTURE.md && cp /tmp/adr.bak docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md
echo "--- restored ---"
git status --short docs/

content.text:
Confirmed. My earlier perl had a parenthesis-spacing mismatch; with the exact phrase removed (while the unrelated line-129 "off-box" remains), the test correctly fails — proving the old loose `"off-box" in text` check would have passed but the new exact-phrase guard discriminates.

All three regression tests are revert-discriminating, currently pass, and match the live docs. Docs are clean/restored.

Summary of verification:
- F-038 makes three docs-drift guards two-sided. All assertions match current docs and pass.
- Empirical revert-discrimination confirmed for all three: deleting the high-stakes "not metered, but off-box" caveat (leaving the unrelated SaaS off-box mention) fails `test_data_sovereignty...`; removing "not shipped by the engine" fails `test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest`; dropping `baseline`/reintroducing "four extras" fails `test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra`.
- The fix is test-only (the docs were already corrected in pass-2 #136/#151/#154); F-038 closes the gap that the guards were presence-only and could pass against a regression that added a stale claim. Idiomatic, no regression. Full suite reported green in the commit (498 passed).

input.reasoning:
F-038 converts three docs-drift guards in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_docs_drift.py from one-sided (presence-only) to two-sided, closing the gap that a regression ADDING a stale claim alongside corrected text passed CI.

Appropriate: The change is test-only and idiomatic (string presence/absence assertions consistent with the existing file). The underlying docs were already corrected in pass-2 (#136/#151/#154); F-038 correctly hardens the guards rather than re-editing docs. No regression — full suite was green in the commit (498 passed) and the three named tests pass now (verified: 3 passed).

Complete + discriminating: I empirically revert-tested all three.
1) test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier: GOVERNANCE.md line 129 has an unrelated "off-box" (SaaS disqualification) and line 132 has the high-stakes caveat "not metered, but off-box". Deleting only the line-132 phrase (old loose `"off-box" in text` still passed) now fails on the exact-phrase assert — confirmed FAIL.
2) test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest: removing "not shipped by the engine" from ARCHITECTURE.md (BaselineBuilder still present, so old single-substring check passed) now fails — confirmed FAIL.
3) test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra: dropping `baseline` and reintroducing stale "four extras" now fails — confirmed FAIL.

All assertions match the live docs (GOVERNANCE.md:132, ARCHITECTURE.md:22, src/assay_engine/__init__.py:16-17, ADR-0006 lines 21-22). Docs were restored to clean after testing. Both appropriate and complete.

content.text:
Verification complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED.

F-038 is both appropriate and complete. The fix makes three docs-drift guards two-sided, and I empirically verified all three regression tests discriminate the fix (each fails when the corresponding doc correction is reverted while a same-file decoy substring remains — exactly the scenario the old loose checks missed).
